topic: Verification c8: ISS-2190 patch closure — finding + detail_verdict_text widened from 3–6 to 3–9 µGal
our_argument_summary: Membach SG 1999 null is cited by the dome as confirming, but applying the dome's own κ = 1.677 nT/µGal (WIN-012) to the documented 1999 European-eclipse magnetic perturbation (multi-site 5–15 nT envelope per Strestík 2001, Hvožďara & Prigancová 2002, Curto et al. 2006) predicts a contact-phase Δg of order 3–9 µGal — well above the SG noise floor (0.01–0.1 µGal). The SG recorded zero. Verdict: Refuted by Data. Defense carve-outs are addressed structurally in Part 6.14 (κ Coupling Cluster) as a vise: every carve-out that exempts 1999 from κ also destroys PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal August 2026 derivation, because κ is the only published mechanism producing that target.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No automation. monitor.py contains no entry for the Membach 1999 null and no live fetch for SG residual data. The κ value (WIN-012) is hardcoded into monitor.py as a constant.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Not applicable — this WIN is not monitored at all by monitor.py. Verification is post-hoc literature reference.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None in code. Defense carve-outs (Chebychev band-stop, Devonian carbonate vs basement granite, mechanism shift) are narrative, not code.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None for this verification cycle. Patches were limited to two prose-level string substitutions in data/wins.json; no code path touched.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py has no domain key, no API call, and no comparison logic for the Membach SG 1999 null. The WIN appears in the dome predictions page but the automation does not validate it.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: There is no standard-physics prediction of an eclipse-coincident contact-phase gravity pulse from magnetic perturbation. The dome originates this prediction via its own published κ. The standard-physics expectation is null, which is what was observed; the κ-cluster is what fails.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The Mansinha et al. 2001 (J. Geodet. Soc. Japan, DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387) Membach SG eclipse measurement was publicly indexed years before the dome model existed. The dome adopted the known null result and labeled it confirming. monitor.py contains no commit-timestamped entry for the value, making even the weaker commit-timestamp test inapplicable.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The 1999 SG null is a measurement that pre-existed the dome. The dome does not derive the null from disc-geometry parameters; it adopts the published null and labels it confirming. κ itself (which we apply against the null) is derived from a 2017 ÷ 1997 ratio per WIN-012, not from disc geometry — see WIN-012 self-contradiction.

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN κ-precision discrepancy (pre-existing, not introduced by ISS-2190): WIN-012 `finding` carries κ = 1.67 nT/µGal (two decimals, a truncation of −10.9 ÷ −6.5 = 1.6769...), while WIN-013 and WIN-014 uniformly cite κ = 1.677 nT/µGal (three decimals) across all four narrative fields. A defender doing side-by-side reading of the κ-cluster sees: 'WIN-012 says your derived κ is 1.67. WIN-013/014 plug in 1.677. Which is your published constant?' Arithmetic impact is null — 5/1.67 = 2.99 µGal vs 5/1.677 = 2.98 µGal (0.42% delta) — and the 3–9 µGal predicted range survives either value. But the appearance of unpatched precision is the same 'calculator-armed defender finds it in 30 seconds' pattern that drove ISS-2190. NOTE: this is already tracked under ISS-2170 (WIN-012 finding rewrite, status=fixed, c10 verification queued at qid 395) — the rewrite preserved the truncated '1.67'. Surface this for ISS-2170's verification rather than opening a new issue against WIN-013.
holes_found.recommendation: Defer to ISS-2170 c10 re-verification (qid 395, target WIN-012). Recommend that verification cycle update WIN-012 finding to 'κ = 1.677 nT/µGal' (or include the full ratio −10.9 ÷ −6.5 = 1.677) so the κ-cluster reads with one precision throughout. No action needed on WIN-013 — its fields are internally consistent and arithmetically correct at the cited precision.

kernel_of_truth.description: ISS-2190 was a real partial-patch slip and the decider's self-apply closed it cleanly. Confirmed by direct field inspection (wins.json:finding, wins.json:detail_verdict_text, wins.json:detail_evidence, wins.json:tldr_evidence): all four narrative fields that previously carried a µGal range now uniformly say '3–9 µGal' (regex match: 3[–-]9 = 4/4 hits; 3[–-]6 = 0/4 hits). The 5–15 nT ΔB envelope cited in detail_evidence (Strestík 2001; Hvožďara & Prigancová 2002, DOI 10.1029/2002JA009260; Curto et al. 2006) is now arithmetically consistent with the predicted Δg range through κ = 1.677 nT/µGal: 5/1.677 ≈ 2.98 µGal (rounds down to 3) and 15/1.677 ≈ 8.94 µGal (rounds up to 9). Build drift check: docs/index.html (lines 1418, 1575, 1585) carries '3–9 µGal' in 10 places and '3–6 µGal' in 0 places — build was regenerated against the patched data. The patch is structurally complete and the refutation force is unchanged (3 vs 6 vs 9 µGal are all 30–900× above the 0.01–0.1 µGal Mansinha 2001 noise floor).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The κ-cluster verdict (Refuted by Data) does not turn on the µGal range — it turns on the existence of a non-zero predicted pulse against a measured zero. The 3–9 µGal closure restores cross-field consistency but does not change the structural argument: under the dome's own published coupling κ (wins.json:012.finding) and its own cited ΔB envelope (data/wins.json:013.detail_evidence sentence 3), the SG measurement should have been a few µGal; it was 0.0 µGal at 0.01–0.1 µGal precision. The Membach SG result remains the empirical pillar; this cycle was a verification of text hygiene, not a re-derivation. The c7 reviewer's prediction — 'this is exactly the kind of slip a calculator-armed defender finds in 30 seconds' — is the right framing for why these consistency-patches matter even when they don't move the verdict needle.

deeper_analysis:
Verification scope was the ISS-2190 two-string patch on data/wins.json (finding + detail_verdict_text widened 3–6 → 3–9 µGal). Step-by-step audit:

(1) Direct field check: 4/4 fields that previously carried a µGal range now agree on 3–9 µGal. The c7 review's '5 field-text loci' phrasing was loose — only 4 fields carry an explicit µGal range (finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, tldr_evidence). tldr_verdict and claim do not state a µGal value (claim is the 15-char topic label 'Membach SG null'; tldr_verdict speaks structurally — 'The dome's own published coupling predicts a measurement that did not occur'). No inconsistency.

(2) Arithmetic: κ = 1.677 nT/µGal applied to 5–15 nT ΔB envelope gives 2.98–8.95 µGal, conservatively rounded to 3–9 µGal. The earlier 3–6 µGal corresponds to a 5–10 nT ΔB range, inconsistent with the explicitly cited 5–15 nT envelope in detail_evidence. Post-patch, prediction and citation match.

(3) Cross-WIN κ-cluster (WIN-010/011/012/013/014/058b): WIN-013 and WIN-014 are now uniformly 3–9 µGal with κ = 1.677 nT/µGal and ΔB = 5–15 nT cited in matching fields. The two parallel SG-null refutations now read as a clean cross-eclipse pair. Pre-existing minor discrepancy: WIN-012 finding says κ = 1.67 nT/µGal (truncation, ISS-2170 territory).

(4) Build drift: docs/index.html shows 10 instances of '3–9 µGal' and 0 of '3–6 µGal' (lines 1418 — summary table row, 1575 — Evidence TLDR, 1585 — Verdict TLDR, plus the matching WIN-014 rendering at 1593+). The publish pipeline regenerated the HTML against the patched data.

(5) Verdict integrity: 'Refuted by Data' continues to fit. The empirical pillar (Membach SG zero across C1/C4) and the dome-internal pillar (κ × ΔB → 3–9 µGal predicted) remain in direct conflict.

(6) Defender-mode pivot space: the most plausible pivot a dome defender could mount against this WIN is to argue that the cited 5–15 nT ΔB envelope describes central European observatories collectively, not the magnetic station nearest Membach specifically. detail_evidence already concedes the envelope is a multi-site range and addresses this by citing three independent studies with overlapping ranges. A narrower defense — 'cite the ΔB at Manhay or Dourbes specifically' — would require us to find the per-station 1999 Belgian readings, which are not in the current citation set. This is a known soft spot but it was not introduced by ISS-2190 and is not in scope for this verification cycle. Flag for analyst future-look at the κ-cluster section, not for this review's verdict.

(7) PROP-009 discipline: queue_id 401 copied verbatim from priority-queue.json; queue_pushed_at 2026-05-24T21:20:59Z copied verbatim. Filename WIN-013.c8.json increments c7. No prior-cycle holes carried forward (the c7 hole was the one ISS-2190 closed); unresolved_prior_cycle = [].

citations_verified: Mansinha, Ducarme, Hinderer, Meurers & van Ruymbeke (2001) — DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387 — alternative-anchor citation for the four-station SG ensemble (Membach, Uccles, Strasbourg, Vienna). Plausible Japanese geodesy society journal DOI structure; no change since c6 introduced it as the replacement for the broken dome-side Van Camp 10.1023 DOI.

Hvožďara & Prigancová (2002, JGR 107, "Geomagnetic effects due to an eclipse-induced low-conductivity ionospheric spot") — DOI 10.1029/2002JA009260 — JGR Space Physics DOI structure plausible; cited for ΔX = −5 to −8, ΔY = +12 to +15 nT range.
Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi (2013, Rep. Prog. Phys. 76, 046101) — DOI 10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101 — Rep. Prog. Phys. DOI structure plausible; cited for SG residual noise floor of 0.1–1 µGal.
citations_unchecked: Strestík (2001) Springer book chapter (10.1007/978-94-010-0800-6_55) — link target appears plausible (Springer DOI for a book series chapter); not re-verified this cycle since no change to the citation since c6.

Curto et al. (2006) — agupubs DOI not surfaced in current detail_evidence text excerpt; cited as one of three sources in the ΔB-envelope sentence. Not re-verified this cycle since no change to the citation since c6.

Verdict (Refuted by Data) holds. recommended_action: no_change for WIN-013 specifically. One minor finding flagged: WIN-012 finding still says κ = 1.67 (truncation) while WIN-013/014 say 1.677 — defer to ISS-2170's queued c10 verification (qid 395) rather than opening a new issue.
